ogTitle: Squeaking Point vs St Helens: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Squeaking Point and St Helens.
heading: Squeaking Point vs St Helens — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Squeaking Point, Tasmania and St Helens, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Squeaking Point has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to St Helens (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Squeaking Point or St Helens safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Squeaking Point has a flood risk score of 22/100, while St Helens scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Squeaking Point scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. St Helens scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Squeaking Point or St Helens?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Squeaking Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
